description: Control Home Assistant: read/write entity states, call services, manage automations/scripts/scenes, publish MQTT, control Modbus, render templates, view history/logbook, and manage the HA system. Every call requires ha_url (the base URL of the HA instance).

conditional_requirements: call_service requires domain and service; data is optional JSON object
fire_event requires event_type; event_data is optional
get_history requires entity_id; hours_back defaults to 24
get_logbook accepts optional entity_id and hours_back
get_calendar_events requires entity_id, start, and end (ISO 8601)
toggle_automation requires entity_id; enabled defaults to true
modbus_write requires unit, address, value, and write_type (coil or holding); hub is optional for multi-hub setups
reload_config_entry requires entry_id (the HA integration config entry id)
get_states accepts optional max_items (1..=500) to cap the entity list for small LLM context budgets (reduces context usage, not network traffic)
get_error_log accepts optional tail_lines (>=1) to return only the last N log lines (reduces LLM context usage, not network traffic, since HA has no server-side tail)
notes: ha_url is required on every call — ask the user for their Home Assistant URL if unknown.
Use get_states with domain_filter to discover entity IDs.
Use get_services to discover available service domains and names.
MQTT: use mqtt_publish or call_service(mqtt, publish).
Modbus: use modbus_write or call_service(modbus, write_register/write_coil).
restart_ha restarts the Home Assistant instance — use with caution.
reload_* actions apply YAML edits without a full restart (reload_core_config, reload_automations, reload_scripts, reload_scenes, reload_themes, reload_config_entry).

auth.instructions:
Create a long-lived access token in Home Assistant:
1. Open your HA instance in a browser
2. Go to your Profile page (click your name in the sidebar)
3. Scroll to 'Long-Lived Access Tokens'
4. Click 'Create Token', give it a name, and copy the token
